mission.title: kyverno: feat: Use standard selector for validationFailureActionOverrides
mission.description:
## Explanation

This PR attempts to resolve https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno/issues/4254.

Modify ValidationFailureActionOverrides
- Add `NamespaceSelector`
- Generate relative manifests
- Implement namespace labels matching logic in engineResponse
- Add test cases

## Related issue

https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno/issues/4254

## Milestone of this PR

## Proposed Changes

Convert `ValidationFailureActionOverrides` spec:
- Add `NamespaceSelector` ([k8s/metav1.LabelSelector](https://pkg.go.d

steps.title: Understand the problem
steps.description:
## Explanation

This PR attempts to resolve https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno/issues/4254.

Modify ValidationFailureActionOverrides
- Add `NamespaceSelector`
- Generate relative manifests
- Implement

steps.title: Apply the configuration
steps.description:
Apply the following configuration to your cluster:
```yaml
validationFailureActionOverrides:
    - action: audit     # Action to apply
      namespaces:
        - dev
      namespaceSelector:       # List of affected namespaces
        matchExpressions:
          - key: "kubernetes.io/metadata.name"
            operator: In
            values:
              - "default"
```

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
Yes, validating selectors seems impossible to me.
First, because it depends on the data in the cluster but validation happens only once at admission time.
Because multiple selectors can be totally unrelated and still match a given namespace.

It looks like we need more work to specify the expected behaviour here.
TBH I wouldn't focus too much on validation but instead on the expected behaviour.

> One solution could be to take the first match but this can be confusing.

I don't think it's a smart idea.
Maybe we can give priority to `enforce` in case the namespace matches both `audit` and `enforce` ?

If `namespaces` and `namespaceSelector` are both defined I would combine them with an AND (combining with an OR is possible by defining two entries).
codeSnippets:
validationFailureActionOverrides:
    - action: audit     # Action to apply
      namespaces:
        - dev
      namespaceSelector:       # List of affected namespaces
        matchExpressions:
          - key: "kubernetes.io/metadata.name"
            operator: In
            values:
              - "default"

validationFailureActionOverrides:
    - action: audit     # Action to apply
      namespaces:
        - dev
      namespaceSelector:       # List of affected namespaces
        matchExpressions:
          - key: "kubernetes.io/metadata.name"
            operator: In
            values:
              - "default"

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: roles-dictionary
  namespace: default
data:
  allowed-roles: "[\"cluster-admin\", \"cluster-operator\", \"tenant-admin\"]"

name: prepend-image-registry
policies:
  - prepend_image_registry.yaml
resources:
  - resource.yaml
variables: values.yaml
results:
  - policy: prepend-registry
    rule: prepend-registry-containers
    resource: mypod
    # if mutate rule
    patchedResource: patchedResource01.yaml
    kind: Pod
    result: pass

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kyverno installed or the issue environment reproducible.
